definition: AI-to-aggregate-productivity transmission requires 5-15 years for visibility, not the 2-3 years priced by equities. Four independent frameworks converge on this timeline: generalist_01's 4-stage transmission, generalist_02's 3-phase decomposition (with reorganization being 60-70% of total delay), equity_analyst_01's 4-gate framework, and equity_analyst_02's 3-bottleneck model. Historical compression trend extrapolates to AI ~5-12 years. Only 15-25% probability assigned to the 2-3 year timeline.
evidence: Four independent analytical frameworks converge on 5-15 year timeline
Historical compression trend: electricity ~30yr, IT ~15yr, internet ~7-12yr
15-25% probability assigned to 2-3yr timeline by both equity analysts independently
pair_0: 'convergence through different analytical routes strengthens confidence'
open_questions: Can AI's software-native deployment and recursive reorganization capability compress below 5 years?
Is the reorganization phase (60-70% of delay) genuinely compressible by AI-assisted management restructuring?
Does sectoral sequencing (finance first, government last) create measurable cross-sector divergence before aggregate visibility?
